metadata.title: Build a Decoherence T2 Decay Curve with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = coherence decay curve; CRIMSON = T2 marker/annotations. INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01-B04=T2 decoherence simulation; B05=brand outro.

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'MedhavyOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B05: palette=claude but the outro is 'MedhavyOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

lines: — the perfect name for our AI-powered

beats.narration_text: The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, qubit decoherence. T-two equals one microsecond. Plot the off-diagonal density matrix element rho-zero-one of t, which starts at one and decays as e to the minus t over T-two. Mark the time T-two on the axis. Three things: the rule, the numbers, what to render.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  qubit dephasing, T₂ = 1 μs
  ρ₀₁(t) = e^(−t/T₂), t ∈ [0, 4 μs]
  mark 1/e at t=T₂; label ρ₀₁(T₂)=0.368"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find this line: rho equals e to the minus t over T-two. That is the entire decoherence model. Check: at t equals T-two equals one microsecond, e to the minus one equals zero-point-three-six-seven-nine. That is the one-over-e point — the standard definition of T-two. Thirty seconds. Never skip this.

props.code:
# decoherence.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

T2 = 1e-6          # s  (1 microsecond)
t  = np.linspace(0, 4e-6, 300)  # 0 to 4 μs

# ← the physics: exponential dephasing
rho01 = np.exp(-t / T2)

# Verify at t = T2:
# rho01(T2) = e^(-1) = 0.3679  (1/e point)
# T2 = 1/gamma_dephasing
print(f'rho01 at T2: {np.exp(-1):.4f}')  # 0.3679

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: at t equals T-two equals one microsecond, the curve reads zero-point-three-six-seven-nine — one over e, not one half, not one over ten. That is the T-two definition. Second: the curve is purely exponential — no oscillation, no plateau. This is dephasing only. The qubit's z-component is untouched; only the coherence in the x-y plane vanishes. Both checks pass. Ready to iterate.

Iterate: claude — update decoherence dot py — double the dephasing rate gamma. Watch T-two halve to zero-point-five microseconds. The curve drops to one-over-e twice as fast. One parameter, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, verify, change. The loop is the skill.

props.command:
claude "update decoherence.py:
  → double gamma: T₂ = 0.5 μs
  → overlay new decay on same axes
  → label: T₂ ∝ 1/γ (double γ → half T₂)"

beats.narration_text: Medhavy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at Medhavy A-I. Medhavy dot com.
